purpose: Role roster for adversarial SCBE patent drafting rounds.
authority_rule: Only the umpire rulebook and official sources are authority. Announcer and crowd responses are explanatory/advisory signals only.

roles.job: Explain the round in plain language, summarize what happened, and keep the patent game readable.
roles.prompt_contract: Summarize the claim round for a technical inventor. Do not make legal conclusions. Identify the ball, the pitch, the hit, the call, and the next action.

roles.job: Apply the rulebook: 101, 102, 103, 112 written description, 112 enablement, definiteness, filing-procedure boundaries.
roles.prompt_contract: Call PRIMARY, DEPENDENT, REVIEW, CONTINUATION, DROP, or STRIKE using only the rulebook and cited evidence.

roles.job: Define the feature cleanly, tie it to code/spec support, and prevent unsupported overclaiming.
roles.prompt_contract: State what the feature does, what data it receives, what transform it performs, what state it changes, and where support is found.

roles.job: Broaden claim language while staying inside support, enablement, and practical-application boundaries.
roles.prompt_contract: Broaden the feature without relying on unsupported terms, guarantees, or product-only hype.

roles.job: Check whether the system can be made and used from the current disclosure and implementation evidence.
roles.prompt_contract: Identify implemented support, missing support, and whether examples are working or prophetic.

roles.job: Check whether the formulas are bounded, meaningful, and claim-safe.
roles.prompt_contract: Flag undefined variables, unbounded claims, unsupported guarantees, and places where the formula needs a fallback embodiment.

roles.job: Provide many cheap outside reactions: confusing language, obvious design-arounds, unclear terms, and naive-reader interpretations.
roles.prompt_contract: Give a short opinion only. You are not authority. Look for confusion, overclaiming, missing support, and plain-language design-arounds.

small_model_pool.candidate_remote_class: any free or low-cost OpenAI-compatible small model endpoint configured by environment variable

round_output_schema.umpire_call: PRIMARY | DEPENDENT | REVIEW | CONTINUATION | DROP | STRIKE
